<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get the observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer observes target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify the target of a change
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager &threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 3. Enable thread safety if requested
    if (enableSafety) {
        threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    // 4. Return thread safety status
    return threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

   - `reaction::React::get`: to get the current value of the reactive variable.

  2. Use `reaction::var` to create a reactive variable from `initialValue`.

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // 创建响应式变量
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 执行原子加法操作
    reactiveVar.atomicAddAssign(addValue);
    
    // 获取最终值并返回
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现

/**
 * Function: Test reactive calculation with dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @return int The result of (a + b) * 2
 */
int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    // 创建响应式变量
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // 创建响应式计算，依赖于 var_a 和 var_b
    auto calc_result = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) {

    }, var_a, var_b);
    
    // 返回计算结果
    return calc_result.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // 1. Create observer node
    reaction::ObserverNode node;
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set node name
    graph.setName(&node, name);
    
    // 4. Return node name
    return graph.getName(&node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    This function returns a `std::shared_ptr<ObserverNode>`.

 2. Get the observer graph instance: We can get the singleton instance of the graph by calling `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()`.

 3. Create a batch ID: We can generate a unique batch ID by calling `reaction::ObserverGraph::createBatchId()`.

 4. Register nodes to the batch: We need to register each node to the batch. The function `reaction::ObserverGraph::addNodeToBatch` takes a node and a batch ID.

 5. Verify nodes are in batch: We can check if a node is in a batch by calling `reaction::ObserverNode::isInActiveBatch()`. This function returns true if the node is in an active batch.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return bool Whether batch operations work correctly
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // 1. 创建观察节点
    auto node1 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    auto node2 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    
    // 2. 获取观察图实例
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. 创建批处理ID
    const void* batchId = graph.createBatchId();
    
    // 4. 注册节点到批处理
    graph.addNodeToBatch(node1, batchId);
    graph.addNodeToBatch(node2, batchId);
    
    // 5. 验证节点是否在批处理中
    bool inBatch1 = node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool inBatch2 = node2->isInActiveBatch();
    
    if (!inBatch1 || !inBatch2) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 6. 取消注册批处理
    graph.unregisterActiveBatch(batchId);
    
    // 7. 验证节点是否不再在批处理中
    bool notInBatch1 = !node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool notInBatch2 = !node2->isInActiveBatch();
    
    // 8. 返回成功状态
    return notInBatch1 && notInBatch2;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 We are provided with the necessary classes and methods from the reaction library.

 Steps in code:

 1. Create two nodes: one for the observer and one for the target.
    We'll create them as `TestObserverReset` instances.

 2. Get the observer graph instance using `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()`.

 3. Add an observer relationship: the target observes the observer.
    We can do this by calling `observerGraph.addObserver(&target, &observer)`.

 4. Notify the target: call `target.valueChanged(true)`. Then check that the observer's `m_callCount` is 1 and `m_changed` is true.

 5. Record the call count (we can store it in a variable).

 6. Reset the observer: call `observer.reset()`.

 7. Notify the target again: call `target.valueChanged(true)`.

 8. Check that the observer's `m_callCount` is now the same as before (so it didn't increment) and that `m_changed` is false.

 9. Return true if all checks pass, false otherwise.

 However, note that the `valueChanged` method of the target node will propagate the change to its observers.
 We are using the `TestObserverReset` class which overrides `valueChanged` and increments the call count.

 Important: We must ensure that the observer is added to the graph and that the target is properly set up to notify the observer.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (target observes observer)
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Test failed
    }
    
    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // Reset observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != initialCallCount) {
        return false; // Test failed
    }
    
    return true; // Test passed
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    // Public reset method to reset test state
    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test node closure functionality
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the observer node
 * @return bool Whether node closure worked correctly (relationships cleaned up)
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("observer");
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    graph.addOneObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->notify();
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Reset observer state
    observer->reset();
    
    // Close observer node
    observer->close();
    
    // Notify target again
    target->notify();
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Set up initial relationship: source observes target
    graph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // Notify target and verify relationship works
    target->valueChanged();

    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!source->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return success;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    observers.reserve(observerNames.size());
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {totalCalls, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // Reset nodes to ensure clean state
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(target);
    graph.addNode(observer1);
    graph.addNode(observer2);
    
    // Set up chain relationships: target -> observer1 -> observer2
    target->addOneObserver(observer1);
    observer1->addOneObserver(observer2);
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Verify both observers were notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->getCallCount() > 0;
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->getCallCount() > 0;
    
    // Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // Chain status: both observers were notified and observer2 was notified after observer1
    bool chainStatus = observer1Notified && observer2Notified && (observer1->getCallCount() < observer2->getCallCount());
    
    return {totalCalls, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

/**

    });

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive update chain with multiple dependent calculations
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param newA New value for a
 * @return int The result of the final calculation after updating a
 */
int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var1 = reaction::var(a);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation: sum of var1 and var2
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var1, var2);

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);

}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto info = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return "Name: " + person.getName() + ", Age: " + std::to_string(person.getAge());
    });
    
    // Update name field
    person.setName(newName);
    
    // Update age field
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return updated info
    return info();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recompute_count = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that depends on a and b
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recompute_count++;
        return a() + b();
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int count_after_non_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Create batch context and execute batch updates
    auto batch = reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    });
    batch.execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after batch updates
    int count_after_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sum_calc(), count_after_non_batch, count_after_batch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

  - We'll retrieve the names of each node (varA, varB, calculation, action) using the `getName` method.
  - Finally, we'll combine these names into a single string and return it.

 Note: The `reaction` library provides:
   - `reaction::var` to create a reactive variable
   - `reaction::calc` to create a calculation
   - `reaction::action` to create an action
   - Each of these functions can take a `Name` parameter to set the name.

 However, note that the provided code for `var`, `calc`, and `action` shows that they have an optional `name` parameter.

 We are to use the `getName` method to get the name of each node.

 Let's assume the following names:
   - varA: "Variable A"
   - varB: "Variable B"
   - calculation: "Calculation"
   - action: "Action"

 We'll then combine the names in the order: varA, varB, calculation, action.

 Example of expected output string: "Variable A, Variable B, Calculation, Action"

 Implementation:

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA).setName("Variable A");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB).setName("Variable B");
    
    // Create calculation with name
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&varA, &varB]() {
        return varA() + varB();
    }).setName("Calculation");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto myAction = reaction::action([&varA, &varB]() {
        varA.value(varA() + 1);
        varB.value(varB() + 1);
    }).setName("Action");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string nameVarA = varA.getName();
    std::string nameVarB = varB.getName();
    std::string nameCalc = calculation.getName();
    std::string nameAction = myAction.getName();
    
    // Return combined names string
    return nameVarA + ", " + nameVarB + ", " + nameCalc + ", " + nameAction;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    double lastProcessedValue = 0.0;
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto statusCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        double currentValue = sensor.get();
        double delta = std::abs(currentValue - lastProcessedValue);
        
        // Update recomputation count only if delta exceeds threshold
        if (delta > diffThreshold) {
            recomputeCount++;
        }
        
        lastProcessedValue = currentValue;
        
        // Determine status based on level threshold
        if (currentValue > levelThreshold) {
            return std::string("HIGH");
        } else {
            return std::string("NORMAL");
        }
    });
    
    // 4. Apply filter
    statusCalc.filter([diffThreshold](double newValue, double oldValue) {
        return std::abs(newValue - oldValue) > diffThreshold;
    });
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount;
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc.get();
    
    // 10. Return final status and counts
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profile(initialScore, true);
    
    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance and register owner node
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance and bind fields to owner node
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile.getId(), &ownerNode);
    
    // Step 5: Update score field
    profile.score() += scoreDelta;
    
    // Step 6: Record notification count after score update
    int afterScoreUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 7: Update active field
    profile.active() = false;
    
    // Step 8: Record notification count after status update
    int afterStatusUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 9: Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{afterScoreUpdate, afterStatusUpdate};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    // Create RecordingNode instances
    auto sensor1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto sensor2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(sensor1);
    graph.registerNode(sensor2);
    
    // Set up initial dependency: sensor2 observes sensor1
    graph.addObserver(sensor2, sensor1);
    
    // Save node state for rollback
    graph.saveNodeStateForRollback(sensor2);
    
    // Replace dependencies transactionally: 
    // Create a new node and set sensor2 to observe it instead of sensor1
    auto newSensor = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    graph.registerNode(newSensor);
    
    // Remove the old observer (sensor1) from sensor2
    graph.removeObserver(sensor2, sensor1);
    
    // Add the new observer (newSensor) to sensor2
    graph.addObserver(sensor2, newSensor);
    
    // Trigger notification on second sensor
    sensor2->valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after second binding
    int afterSecondBinding = sensor2->notificationCount();
    
    // Execute rollback
    graph.rollbackNodeState(sensor2);
    
    // Trigger notification on first sensor
    sensor1->valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after rollback
    int afterRollback = sensor2->notificationCount();
    
    return RollbackObservation{afterSecondBinding, afterRollback};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: variable + increment
    auto reactiveExpr = reaction::create(reactiveVar + increment);
    
    // Create reactive calculation: variable squared
    auto reactiveCalc = reaction::create([&reactiveVar]() { 
        return reactiveVar.get() * reactiveVar.get(); 
    });
    
    // Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.value(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr.get();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc.get();
    
    return {initialExprValue, updatedExprValue, squaredValue};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    const std::string& name() const { return m_name; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct");
    auto transitive = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive");
    
    // Get observer graph
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(direct);
    graph.registerNode(transitive);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    source->addOneObserver(direct);
    
    // Set up transitive relationship (transitive observes direct)
    direct->addOneObserver(transitive);
    
    // Collect observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(source, observers);
    
    // Prepare summary
    ObserverCollectionSummary summary;
    summary.totalObservers = observers.size();
    summary.containsDirectObserver = observers.contains(direct);
    summary.containsTransitiveObserver = observers.contains(transitive);
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 We need to implement the function `test_thread_manager_multithread` in the namespace `test_reaction`.
 The function should return a `ThreadSafetyStatus` struct with the following fields:
   - safetyBefore: the thread safety status before creating the worker thread

 Steps:
 1. Get the thread manager instance (using `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`)
 2. Reset the thread manager state (using `resetForTesting()`)
 3. Register the current thread (using `registerThread()`)
 4. Get the thread count and safety status before creating the worker thread:
      threadCountBefore = `getThreadCount()`
      safetyBefore = `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 5. Create and start a worker thread that also registers itself (by calling `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread()` inside the thread)
 6. Wait for the worker thread to finish
 7. Get the thread count and safety status after the worker thread has been joined:
      threadCountAfter = `getThreadCount()`
      safetyAfter = `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 8. Return the ThreadSafetyStatus struct with the collected data.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count and safety status before
    size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 5. Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([&threadManager]() {
        // Worker thread registers itself
        threadManager.registerThread();
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread count and safety status after
    size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Return thread safety status
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {

    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and increments recomputeCount
    auto calcResult = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // 4. Record recompute count before batch
    int beforeBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    // 5. Execute batch updates
    auto batch = reaction::batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.value(reactiveVar.get() + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.value(reactiveVar.get() + deltaB);
    });
    batch.execute();
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after batch
    int afterBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Get final value
    int finalValue = calcResult.get();
    
    // 8. Return batch execution result
    return BatchExecuteResult{beforeBatch, afterBatch, finalValue};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // 3. Create calculation that increments the recompute count
    auto calcResult = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar() * 2; // Example calculation
    });
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    auto batch = reaction::batch([&]() {
        // Update the reactive variable within the batch
        reactiveVar.value(updatedValue);
    });
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 10. Ensure calculation is evaluated (recomputeCount should be at least 1)
    // 11. Return batch lifecycle result
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        closedInitially,
        closedAfterClose,
        recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        // Implementation as per requirement
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // Step 1: Create CacheProbeNode instances
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Register nodes (automatically done when adding observers)
    
    // Step 4: Set up direct observer relationship
    nodeA->addObserver(nodeB);
    
    // Step 5: Set up transitive observer relationship
    nodeB->addObserver(nodeC);
    nodeC->addObserver(nodeD);
    
    // Step 6: Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Step 7: Populate cache data
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observers);
    
    // Step 8: Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Step 9: Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // Step 10: Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Step 11: Close all nodes
    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();
    nodeD->close();
    
    // Step 12: Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = statsBefore.totalEntries,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = statsAfter.totalEntries,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = statsAfter.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyTransition {
    bool beforeEnable;
    bool afterEnable;
    bool afterReset;
    std::size_t threadCount;
};

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    ThreadSafetyTransition transition;
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count after registration
    transition.threadCount = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    transition.beforeEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    transition.afterEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    transition.afterReset = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return transition;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

  6. Get the updated sum
  7. Return a `ConstVarResult` struct with the initial and updated sums

 Steps in code:
  - We'll create a mutable variable (say `mutableVar`) with the initial value `baseValue`.
  - We'll create a constant variable (say `constVar`) with the value `constValue`.
  - We'll create a calculation (say `sumCalc`) that returns the sum of `mutableVar` and `constVar`.
  - We'll get the initial sum by calling `sumCalc.get()`.
  - Then we update the mutable variable by calling `mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta)`.
  - Then we get the updated sum by calling `sumCalc.get()` again.
  - Return the `ConstVarResult` with the two sums.

 Note: The `reaction` library uses a reactive programming model where calculations are updated automatically when their dependencies change.

 However, note that the constant variable is not expected to change, so the calculation should only depend on the mutable variable after the update.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::constVar(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return mutableVar.get() + constVar.get();
    });
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 4. Store the recompute count after initialization

 6. Store the recompute count after duplicate write
 7. Force evaluation to avoid unused variable warnings (by calling `get` on the calculation)

 - We'll create a reactive variable `v` with the initial value.
 - We'll create a counter, say `count`, initialized to 0.
 - We'll create a calculation `c` that depends on `v` and does: 
        count++ and returns 2 * v.get()
   We use `reaction::AlwaysTrig` as the trigger policy for the calculation.

 - After creating the calculation, we get the current count (which should be 1 because the calculation runs once during initialization) and store it in `recomputeCountAfterInit`.
 - Then we set the value of `v` to the same initial value again (using `v.value(initialValue)`).
 - Then we get the current count again and store it in `recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite`.
 - Then we force evaluate the calculation by calling `c.get()` to avoid unused variable warning.
 - Finally, return the struct with the two counts.

 Note: The `reaction::calc` function template takes a trigger policy as the first template argument. We use `reaction::AlwaysTrig` for this test.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create a counter to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Create an AlwaysTrig calculation that doubles the value and increments counter
    auto calcResult = reaction::calc<reaction::AlwaysTrig>([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar.get() * 2;
    });
    
    // Store recompute count after initialization
    int afterInitCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // Write the same value again
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue);
    
    // Store recompute count after duplicate write
    int afterDuplicateWriteCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // Force evaluation to avoid unused variable warnings
    calcResult.get();
    
    // Return result with recompute counts
    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = afterInitCount,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = afterDuplicateWriteCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;

};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Get thread safety status before guard
    bool safetyBeforeGuard = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 4. Get thread count before guard
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create a worker thread with ThreadRegistrationGuard
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        // ThreadRegistrationGuard will automatically register the worker thread
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g;
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after guard
    bool safetyAfterGuard = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Get thread count after guard
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // 9. Return thread guard state
    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Create a Resource instance with initial value
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Variable to track if operation reported a change
    bool changeReported = false;
    
    // Perform atomic operation that adds delta to the value
    resource.atomicOperation([&delta](int& val) -> bool {
        val += delta;
        return true; // Change is always reported when we modify the value
    }, changeReported);
    
    // Get the final value
    int finalValue = resource.get();
    
    // Return the result
    return ResourceAtomicResult{finalValue, changeReported};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 6. Check if the guard was allocated successfully by checking the `allocated` member of the `StackGuard` object (which is a boolean). Store this in `report.guardAllocated`.
 7. While the guard is active, get the current usage by calling `reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage()` and store in `report.usageDuringGuard`.
 8. Get the statistics by calling `reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats()` and store the `usagePercentage` and `nearLimit` from the returned `Stats` struct.
 9. When the guard goes out of scope (step 10), the destructor will decrement the usage.
10. After the guard is destroyed, get the current usage again and store in `report.usageAfterGuard`.
11. Call `reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring()` again to disable monitoring.
12. Check if monitoring is enabled after the second disable and store in `report.monitoringAfterDisable`.

 However, note that the `StackGuard` constructor and destructor are already defined and we are to use them.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 Important: We are to output the complete contents of the file, which includes the given header and the implementation of the function.

 Let's code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report{};
    
    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Create report struct (done above)
    
    // Step 4: Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Step 5: Create StackGuard in a scope block
    bool guardAllocated = false;
    std::size_t usageDuring = 0;
    double percentage = 0.0;
    bool nearLimit = false;
    
    {
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<64> guard; // Using 64 bytes guard
        
        // Step 6: Check if guard was successfully allocated
        guardAllocated = guard.allocated;
        
        if (guardAllocated) {
            // Step 7: Get current usage during guard
            usageDuring = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
            
            // Step 8: Get statistics
            auto stats = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
            percentage = stats.usagePercentage;
            nearLimit = stats.nearLimit;
        }
    } // Guard goes out of scope here
    
    // Step 10: Exit guard scope (handled by block)
    
    // Step 11: Get current usage after guard
    std::size_t usageAfter = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    
    // Step 12: Disable monitoring again
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Populate report
    report.guardAllocated = guardAllocated;
    report.usageDuringGuard = usageDuring;
    report.usageAfterGuard = usageAfter;
    report.usagePercentage = percentage;
    report.nearLimitDuringGuard = nearLimit;
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    // Step 1: Get thread manager instance
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 2: Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.resetForTesting();

    // Step 8: Exit guard scope

 But we are also going to use the try_lock_no_safety and try_shared_lock_no_safety methods.

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    SharedMutexResult result{};
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create ConditionalSharedMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex sharedMutex;
    
    // 4. Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    result.trySharedWithoutSafety = sharedMutex.try_shared_lock_no_safety();
    
    // 5. Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (result.trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        sharedMutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // 6. Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lockGuard(sharedMutex);
        
        // 7. Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held without safety
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = sharedMutex.try_lock_no_safety();
        
        // 8. Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            sharedMutex.unlock();
        }
    } // 9. Exit guard scope
    
    // 10. Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 11. Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    result.trySharedWithSafety = sharedMutex.try_shared_lock();
    
    // 12. Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (result.trySharedWithSafety) {
        sharedMutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // 13. Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block again
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lockGuard(sharedMutex);
        
        // 14. Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held with safety
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = sharedMutex.try_lock();
        
        // 15. Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            sharedMutex.unlock();
        }
    } // 16. Exit guard scope
    
    // 17. Return shared mutex result
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct TinyPod {
    int a;
    int b;
};

struct MediumPod {
    double values[3];
};

struct LargePod {
    std::byte payload[128];
};

struct SboTraitSummary {
    bool tinyAlwaysSbo;
    bool tinyForceHeap;
    bool mediumConditionalSbo;
    bool mediumForceHeap;
    bool largeForceHeap;
    std::size_t alwaysThreshold;
    std::size_t conditionalThreshold;
    std::size_t maxStackUsage;
};

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;
    
    // Get SBO traits for TinyPod
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for MediumPod
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for LargePod
    summary.largeForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<LargePod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    summary.alwaysThreshold = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::ALWAYS_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::CONDITIONAL_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.maxStackUsage = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE;
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    // Change multiplier to double the original value

    // 9. Update source variable
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue + 10);
    
    // 10. Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calc();
    
    // 11. Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

  - We are in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Implementation:

 We'll create a vector of strings, reserve space equal to the size of the input vector to avoid multiple allocations.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Step 1: Define Counter struct
    struct Counter {
        int value;
        void add(int delta) { value += delta; }
    };
    
    // Step 2: Create reactive variable with Counter
    auto reactive_counter = reaction::var(Counter{initialValue});
    
    // Step 3: Get first pointer
    auto first_ptr = reactive_counter.getPtr();
    
    // Step 4: Call add method through first pointer
    first_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Step 5: Get current value after first add
    int value_after_first = reactive_counter.get().value;
    
    // Step 6: Get second pointer
    auto second_ptr = reactive_counter.getPtr();
    
    // Step 7: Call add method through second pointer
    second_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Step 8: Get current value after second add
    int value_after_second = reactive_counter.get().value;
    
    // Step 9: Check if pointer is stable (same underlying object)
    bool is_stable = (first_ptr.get() == second_ptr.get());
    
    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = value_after_first,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = value_after_second,
        .pointerStable = is_stable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables and set names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0);
    var1.setName(firstName);
    
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0);
    var2.setName(secondName);
    
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0);
    var3.setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into set to order by React::operator<
    std::set<reaction::React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    orderedNames.reserve(reactSet.size());
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique
    bool allUnique = (reactSet.size() == orderedNames.size());
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)), m_notificationCount(0) {
    }
    
    int notifications() const noexcept {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }
    
    void clear() noexcept {
        m_notificationCount = 0;
    }
    
private:
    std::string m_name;
    mutable std::atomic<int> m_notificationCount;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    // Create source and target nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Set up observer relationship (source observes target)
    source->addOneObserver(target);
    
    // Trigger notification on source
    source->notify();
    
    // Check if target was notified before reset
    bool notifiedBefore = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Clear target notification count
    target->clear();
    
    // Reset target node
    target->reset();
    
    // Trigger notification on source again
    source->notify();
    
    // Check if target was notified after reset
    bool notifiedAfter = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Close nodes
    graph.closeNode(source);
    graph.closeNode(target);
    
    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate StackMonitor::canAllocate behaviour across modes.
 *
 * @param largeSize Value used for runtime canAllocate after release
 * @return StackAllocationSnapshot Snapshot of allocation checks before/after guard
 */
inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().setMonitoringEnabled(false);
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Check allocation without monitoring
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(1);
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // Step 4: Enable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().setMonitoringEnabled(true);
    
    // Step 5: Initialize stack monitor again
    reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();

        allocationBlockedAtLimit = !guard.success(); // Allocation blocked if guard fails
    } // Guard goes out of scope here
    
    // Step 11: Check runtime allocation after release
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = reaction::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // Step 12: Return stack allocation snapshot
    return StackAllocationSnapshot{
        .smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        .largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        .runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring,
        .largeAllowedWithMonitoring = largeAllowedWithMonitoring,
        .guardSucceeded = guardSucceeded,
        .allocationBlockedAtLimit = allocationBlockedAtLimit,
        .runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = runtimeAllowedAfterRelease
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(name) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notifications = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field nodes
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Register nodes
    observerGraph.addNode(owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(field);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 5: Register field under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObjField(objectId, field->shared_from_this());
    
    // Step 6: Bind field to owner
    observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner);
    
    // Step 7: Trigger notification on field
    field->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 8: Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = owner->notifications() > 0;
    
    // Step 9: Clear owner notification count
    owner->clear();
    
    // Step 10: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.closeNode(owner);
    
    // Step 11: Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.deleteObjField(objectId);
    
    // Step 12: Attempt to bind field again (should fail due to owner being closed)
    // observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner); // This will likely throw or have no effect
    
    // Step 13: Trigger notification on field again
    field->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Step 14: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = owner->notifications() > 0;
    
    // Step 15: Close nodes
    observerGraph.closeNode(field);
    
    // Step 16: Return result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    // Step 1: Enable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::enableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Get usage statistics before guard
    auto statsBeforeGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 4: Check if template allocation is allowed
    // Using a large type that would exceed the stack limit
    struct LargeType {
        char buffer[reaction::memory::SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE];
    };
    bool templateAllocationAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate<LargeType>();
    
    // Step 5: Check if runtime allocation is allowed
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate(
        reaction::memory::SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE
    );
    
    // Step 6: Create large StackGuard instance
    // Using a size that exceeds the remaining stack space
    constexpr size_t largeSize = reaction::memory::SBOConfig::MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE * 2;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<largeSize> guard;
    
    // Step 7: Check if guard succeeded
    bool guardHeld = guard.success();
    
    // Step 8: Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = false;
    if (guardHeld) {
        allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = !reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate(1);
    }
    
    // Step 9: Get usage statistics after guard
    auto statsAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 10: Return stack limit snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBeforeGuard,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfterGuard,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllocationAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllocationAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 7. Assign a node name (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().setName(resource.getPtr(), nodeName)).
 8. Check if the name was assigned (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().getName(resource.getPtr()) == nodeName).
 9. Close the node (using ObserverGraph::getInstance().closeNode(resource.getPtr())).

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<Void>>();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool uses_sbo = resource->isUsingSBO();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    
    // 4. Check if retrieved value is Void type
    bool is_void = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // 5. Get observer graph instance
    auto &observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Register resource node
    observer_graph.addNode(resource);
    
    // 7. Assign node name
    observer_graph.setName(resource, nodeName);
    
    // 8. Check if name was assigned
    bool name_assigned = (observer_graph.getName(resource) == nodeName);
    
    // 9. Close node
    observer_graph.closeNode(resource);
    
    // 10. Check if name was cleared after close
    bool name_cleared = (observer_graph.getName(resource) == "");
    
    // 11. Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = is_void,
        .usesSboStorage = uses_sbo,
        .nameAssigned = name_assigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = name_cleared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
